page_content:
GVPT205 Special Topics in International Ethics, Conflict, and Immigration (3 Credits) An examination of issues in international ethics, conflict generated at the international level, and problems in immigration policy and law, including theories of rights and immigration, and ideological sources of international violence. Prerequisite: GVPT105. Recommended: GVPT241. Restriction: Must be in College Park Scholars program; and must be in GVPT international Studies program.

GVPT206 Appetite for Change: Politics and the Globalization of Food (3 Credits) An overview of the major forces transforming the food system--political, economic, technological, environmental--and the political debates surrounding them. Credit Only Granted for: GVPT206 or GVPT289A. Formerly: GVPT289A.
